narrative.headline: 2020 Jeep Renegade: engine leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 62 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area at 34%, followed by the electrical system (23%) and the power train (11%). No fires or deaths are reported. Seat belts draw only 6 complaints (10%) but carry the most crashes (3) and injuries (4) of any area, so they are worth a close look.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 21 reports (34%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Owners describe sudden stalling and warning lights, so check for these on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 14 reports (23%), with 1 crash and 1 injury. Worth checking the electronics carefully.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 7 reports (11%), with no crashes, injuries, or fires reported. A recurring but lower-severity complaint area.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 6 reports (10%), linked to 1 crash with no injuries reported. A smaller complaint area worth noting.

issues.title: Seat Belts
issues.blurb: Only 6 reports (10%) but the most serious by outcome: 3 crashes and 4 injuries, the highest of any area here. Worth a careful look.
